description: Confounder: The model applies the default NaN-exclusion rule without accounting for the drop

_meta.explanation: The rule 'In pandas, NaN values are excluded from groupby groups by default, so a groupby aggregation will not produce a group key for NaN.' does NOT hold here. The model applies the default NaN-exclusion rule without accounting for the dropna=False parameter, which causes NaN to be retained as a group key.
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'In pandas, NaN values are excluded from groupby groups by default, so a groupby aggregation will not produce a group key for NaN.' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
